title: MCP server doesn't handle real-time API fallback
evidence: server.py only uses bundled CSV data; no API-Football or TheSportsDB integration
suggestion: Optional enhancement per spec: add real-time data via API-Football (free tier with rate limits) for current season queries

title: 15 MCP tools exposed via FastMCP
evidence: server.py: find_matches, head_to_head, team_record, team_summary, compare_teams, find_players, get_player, club_squad, standings, season_results, list_competitions, competition_stats, biggest_wins, best_records, top_scoring_teams
suggestion: All required capabilities are exposed as callable MCP tools

title: All performance targets met
evidence: README: simple lookups < 10ms, aggregate queries < 5ms; test suite completes in 1.10s
suggestion: Performance is well under the 2s/5s SLA targets

title: Comprehensive test coverage with 52 passing tests
evidence: pytest output: 52 passed in 1.10s; includes BDD scenarios (test_competitions_bdd.py, test_matches_bdd.py, test_players_bdd.py, test_statistics_bdd.py, test_teams_bdd.py) and unit tests
suggestion: Test suite covers all major capabilities and edge cases; 0 skipped tests

title: Intelligent deduplication across overlapping datasets
evidence: data_loader.py: 2019 Brasileirão appears in 3 files; fixtures are keyed by (competition, season, home, away) and deduplicated with ±1-day offset tolerance
suggestion: Ensures accurate aggregates even when the same match exists in multiple source CSVs

title: Robust team name normalization
evidence: normalize.py + test_unit.py: handles state suffixes (Palmeiras-SP), accents (São Paulo), club-type tokens (EC Bahia, Fortaleza FC), and disambiguates distinct Atlético clubs (MG/GO/PR)
suggestion: Comprehensive coverage of real-world naming variations in Brazilian soccer

title: Multiple date format support
evidence: data_loader.py parse_date: handles ISO (2023-09-24), with time (2012-05-19 18:30:00), and Brazilian format (29/03/2003)
suggestion: All source files' date formats are parsed transparently

title: Full UTF-8 support for Portuguese text
evidence: data_loader.py: all CSVs read as UTF-8 with BOM tolerance; test_unit.py includes accent-preserving unit tests (São Paulo, Grêmio, Avaí)

title: Computed standings validated against historical records
evidence: README: 2010→Fluminense, 2015→Corinthians, 2019→Flamengo, 2021→Atlético Mineiro (all correct); avg goals/match ~2.47 matches spec
suggestion: Standings algorithm (3pts win, 1pt draw, ranked by points→wins→GD) is correct

title: All 6 CSV files loaded and queryable
evidence: data/kaggle/: Brasileirao_Matches.csv (4,180 matches), Brazilian_Cup_Matches.csv (1,337), Libertadores_Matches.csv (1,255), BR-Football-Dataset.csv (10,296), novo_campeonato_brasileiro.csv (6,886), fifa_data.csv (18,207 players)
suggestion: Complete coverage of provided datasets with proper attribution (CC BY 4.0, CC0, Apache 2.0)
